plants.title: Delicious Monster
plants.subtitle: Split-leaf philodendron
plants.description: Delicious Monster, called the cerimán or Adam's rib, is a species of climbing plant in the Araceae family, endemic to tropical rainforests from central Mexico to northern Argentina.
plants.origin: Native to tropical forests of southern Mexico, south to Panama.

plants.title: Black Bat Flower
plants.subtitle: Tacca chantrieri
plants.description: One of the most bizarre flowers in the plant kingdom, the Black Bat Flower features dark purple-black petals resembling a bat in flight, with long whisker-like bracteoles that can reach up to 70cm.
plants.origin: Native to tropical regions of Southeast Asia, including Thailand and southern China.

plants.title: Living Stones
plants.subtitle: Lithops
plants.description: Masters of camouflage, Lithops are succulent plants that mimic the appearance of pebbles and stones to avoid being eaten by herbivores in their native desert habitat.
plants.origin: Native to the arid regions of southern Africa, particularly Namibia and South Africa.

plants.title: Rafflesia
plants.subtitle: Rafflesia arnoldii
plants.description: The largest individual flower on Earth, Rafflesia can reach up to 1 meter in diameter and weigh up to 10kg. It produces a rotting flesh smell to attract pollinating flies.
plants.origin: Native to the rainforests of Sumatra and Borneo, Indonesia.

plants.title: Rainbow Eucalyptus
plants.subtitle: Eucalyptus deglupta
plants.description: The only eucalyptus native to the northern hemisphere, its trunk reveals a stunning natural rainbow as patches of outer bark shed annually, exposing bright green inner bark that matures to blue, purple and orange.
plants.origin: Native to the Philippines, Indonesia and Papua New Guinea.

plants.title: Corpse Flower
plants.subtitle: Amorphophallus titanum
plants.description: Famous for producing the world's largest unbranched inflorescence, the Corpse Flower blooms only once every few years and emits a powerful odor of rotting flesh to attract pollinators.
plants.origin: Native to the rainforests of Sumatra, Indonesia.

plants.title: Ghost Orchid
plants.subtitle: Dendrophylax lindenii
plants.description: One of the rarest and most mysterious flowers in the world, the Ghost Orchid has no leaves and appears to float in mid-air. It can only be pollinated by the giant sphinx moth.
plants.origin: Native to Cuba, the Bahamas and the swamps of Florida, USA.

plants.title: Dragon Blood Tree
plants.subtitle: Dracaena cinnabari
plants.description: With its iconic umbrella-shaped canopy and dark red resin known as dragon's blood, this ancient tree is one of the most alien-looking plants on Earth, surviving in one of the world's most isolated archipelagos.
plants.origin: Endemic to the Socotra archipelago, Yemen.

plants.title: Venus Flytrap
plants.subtitle: Dionaea muscipula
plants.description: The most famous carnivorous plant in the world, the Venus Flytrap snaps shut in under a second when its trigger hairs are touched, trapping insects and spiders to digest as a nutrient source.
plants.origin: Native to the subtropical wetlands of North and South Carolina, USA.

plants.title: Welwitschia
plants.subtitle: Welwitschia mirabilis
plants.description: A true living fossil, Welwitschia produces only two leaves throughout its entire life, which can span over 2000 years. These leaves grow continuously and become shredded by the desert winds over centuries.
plants.origin: Endemic to the Namib Desert in Namibia and Angola.
